title: caladium bicolor var. vermitoxicum
intro: Caladium bicolor var. vermitoxicum, also known as the poisonous dragon or caladium, is a species of flowering plant within the alocasia family. It contains toxic compounds, such as calcium oxalate crystals and glycosides, which are traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing pain, and promoting skin health. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often crushed and applied topically, while its roots are used in decoctions and infusions to harness its medicinal properties, but caution is advised due to its toxic nature.
